title: harness/config.json schema
description: Schema for the per-repo harness/config.json. The schema is intentionally permissive about empty strings — an unconfigured command is an improvement opportunity, not a validation failure.

version.description: Schema version, e.g. "0.1". Distinct from the skill version that authored the file.

cli_language.description: Which CLI implementation the skill installed. "wrap" means the wrap-existing branch (harness/bin/harness.sh shim); "" means no runtime was detected and the install completed in degraded mode.

cli_path.description: Path to the installed CLI, e.g. harness/bin/harness.py. Empty when cli_language is "".

commands.description: Each value is the shell command (possibly empty string). Empty commands are skipped by validate and reported as 'unconfigured' by doctor.

url.description: Empty string disables the health subcommand.

validation.description: Step lists per validation tier. Each step name must be a key in commands{} OR one of the well-known names: 'doctor', 'health'. 'run' is allowed in lists but is always skipped at execution time (AC-15 long-running-boot lockdown).

permissions.description: Governs the long-running-boot lockdown. allow_run=false (default) means 'run' is dry-run unless --execute is also passed.

notes.description: Free-form notes the skill writes for posterity. Not parsed.
